summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the Playwright trace viewer HTTP server serves arbitrary files from the host filesystem via the unauthenticated /trace/file?path= endpoint.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the trace viewer server to start and serve the contents of /etc/passwd (or another known file) in response to an unauthenticated HTTP GET request to /trace/file?path=/etc/passwd, demonstrating arbitrary file read.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the Playwright repository, built it from source, started the trace viewer HTTP server on port 9999, and the exploit request to /trace/file?path=/etc/passwd returned the full contents of /etc/passwd with HTTP 200 status, confirming arbitrary file read without authentication.
verification_method: The exploit script sends GET /trace/file?path=/etc/passwd and checks that: (1) the HTTP status code is 200, and (2) the response body contains the string 'root:' which is expected in /etc/passwd. This distinguishes a successful file read from a 404 error, startup failure, or unrelated behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP GET to /trace/file?path=/etc/passwd returned HTTP 200 with the file contents.
The response body contains 'root:' confirming it is the actual /etc/passwd file.
No authentication, cookies, or tokens were required to access the endpoint.
limitations_or_blockers: The build from source step (npm install + npm run build) takes significant time (5-20 minutes depending on system resources).
The trace viewer server needs to be started with --host to bind to a reachable address.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
